subject: english
learning_objectives: Define diphthongs and distinguish them from monophthongs
Identify the eight diphthong sounds in English
Recognise spelling patterns associated with each diphthong

sections.heading: Definition
sections.content: Diphthongs are two-quality (double element) vowel sounds. To realise a diphthong, the speaker must glide from the first sound element to the second. English has eight diphthongs.

sections.heading: The Eight Diphthongs with Spelling Variants
sections.content:
/eɪ/ – 'a' (cake, brake, lake) | 'ai' (braid, sail) | 'ay' (May, day) | 'ey' (they, prey) | 'ei' (rein, reign) | 'ea' (great, break, steak)

/əʊ/ – 'o' (go, rote, note) | 'oa' (goat, road) | 'eo' (hoe, foe) | 'ew' (sew, sewn) | 'au' (chauffeur) | 'oo' (brooch)

/aɪ/ – 'ai' (aisle, naira) | 'ei' (height, either) | 'uy' (buy, guy) | 'ye' (bye, dye) | 'y' (by, sky) | 'ie' (pie, tie, lie)

/aʊ/ – 'ou' (doubt, count, about) | 'au' (Nassau) | 'ow' (how, cow, allow)

/ɔɪ/ – 'oi' (boil, oil, noise) | 'oy' (boy, employ) | 'uoy' (buoy, buoyant)

/ɪə/ – 'e' (era, period) | 'ea' (idea, beard) | 'ere' (here, mere) | 'eer' (peer, sheer) | 'ier' (pier, weir) | 'ir' (fakir)

/eə/ – 'air' (air, fair, hair) | 'are' (care, fare, dare) | 'ear' (bear, tear, wear) | 'ere' (there, where)

/ʊə/ – 'u' (plural, during) | 'ure' (pure, cure, sure) | 'our' (tour) | 'oor' (moor, poor)
